title: dichrophyllum marginatum
intro: Dichrophyllum marginatum, also known as Chilean yerba buena, is a perennial herb native to South America, characterized by its small, ovate leaves and clusters of small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Dichrophyllum marginatum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and act as a natural insect repellent. Herbal preparations of Dichrophyllum marginatum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
